[[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=}} in [[Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Historic Roads]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[en:Nose Highway]]

It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Historic Roads]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[en:Nose Highway]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]]

revision.text:
[[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=}} in [[Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Historic Roads]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[en:Nose Highway]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Historic Roads]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[en:Nose Highway]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
[[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=}} in [[Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Historic Roads]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Transportation in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Transportation in Hyōgo
revision.text:
[[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=}} in [[Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箕面滝}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')

Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Transportation in Osaka]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箕面滝}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:兵庫県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611938]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=}} in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箕面滝}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=|1=Hattori Tenjin-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516907}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Tōkōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526045}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Harada-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409912}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611938]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=|1=Nakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366419}} in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Mount Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箕面滝|1=Minoh Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603215}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=|1=Ryūan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566301}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=|1=Chūgoku Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363429}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=|1=Mikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354715}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Shōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485796}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Okamachi, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11473739}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=|1=Ishibashi, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=|1=Hattori Tenjin-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516907}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Tōkōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526045}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Harada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409912}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōken Line|WD=Q15223648}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=|1=Ishibashi, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=|1=Seigoku kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518413}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=|1=Ikeda no Inokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551659}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=|1=Kikuzumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683022}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Mount Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōken Line|WD=Q15223648}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=|1=Hattori Tenjin-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516907}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Tōkō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526045}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Harada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409912}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōken Line|WD=Q15223648}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=|1=Ishibashi, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=|1=Seigoku kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518413}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=|1=Ikeda no Inokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551659}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=|1=Kikuzumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683022}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Mount Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nose Highway]] [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11611938}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611938]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image: Nose kaido&Harada shrine.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Nose Kaidō near Okamachi, which has become a shopping street. To the left is Harada Shrine.]] The '''Nose Kaidō''' (能勢街道) is an old highway stretching from {{ill|Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|ja|中津 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Kita, Osaka|qq=|1=Nakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366419}} in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]'s Kita Ward, passing through [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], and ending at {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Mount Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}}, where the {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located. From Chōkōji-minami, the highway splits, with the {{ill|Minoh Kaidō|ja|箕面街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} providing a pilgrimage route to sites such as {{ill|Minoh Waterfall|ja|箕面の滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoh Waterfall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箕面滝|1=Minoh Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603215}}, {{ill|Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|ja|瀧安寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuan-ji (Minoh)|qq=|1=Ryūan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566301}}, and [[:en:Katsuō-ji|Katsuo-ji]]. Today, the old road name is also used colloquially for the routes of [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] (Osaka–Toyonaka–Ikeda) and [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] (Ikeda–Tada–Nose–Sasayama), excluding bypasses. == History and Geography == The Nose Kaidō diverged from the {{ill|Chūgoku Kaidō|ja|中国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Kaidō|qq=|1=Chūgoku Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363429}} in Nakatsu, then passed through [[:en:Jūsō|Jūso]], {{ill|Mikuni, Osaka|ja|三国 (大阪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni, Osaka|qq=|1=Mikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354715}}, {{ill|Shōnai, Toyonaka|ja|庄内 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōnai, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Shōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485796}}, {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}}, {{ill|Okamachi, Toyonaka|ja|岡町 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamachi, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Okamachi, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11473739}}, {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=|1=Ishibashi, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}}, Ikeda, Kibe, Furue, [[:en:Tada Station (Hyōgo)|Tada Station (Hyōgo)]], and [[:en:Ichinotorii Station|Ichinotorii Station]], before following the ridgeline to Myōken Mountain. This highway facilitated the transportation of goods such as sake, textiles, and timber from Ikeda and Nose to Osaka. Additionally, rice, chestnuts, charcoal, silver, and copper from [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] were carried along this route. Many significant religious sites lined the highway, including {{ill|Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|ja|服部天神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori Tenjingu Shrine|qq=|1=Hattori Tenjin-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516907}}, {{ill|Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|ja|東光院 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-in (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Tōkō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526045}} (Hagi Temple), {{ill|Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|ja|原田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Shrine (Toyonaka)|qq=|1=Harada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409912}}, and [[:en:Tada Shrine|Tada Shrine]]. Other sites, such as {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} and Katsuo-ji, located off the main route, also attracted pilgrims. In 1876, the Nose Kaidō was designated as one of three first-class main roads in Osaka Prefecture under Daijōkan Order No. 60, which established road classifications. Historically, the route between Okamachi and Ikeda passed through Mount Tonoyama (south of Osaka University's Toyonaka Campus). Due to the steep incline, which hampered the transport of goods, a flatter "New Nose Kaidō" was built in 1894, running from the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Hankyu Toyonaka Station|ja|阪急豊中駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Toyonaka Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Ishibashi. This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu Electric Railway]]'s [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Line]] and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The [[:en:Nose Electric Railway|Nose Electric Railway]]'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōken Line|WD=Q15223648}} also parallels the highway north of [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]]. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around [[:en:Ishibashi handai-mae Station|Ishibashi Handai-mae Station]] have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|qq=|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|qq=|1=Ishibashi, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|qq=|1=Seigoku kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518413}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|qq=|1=Ikeda no Inokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551659}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|qq=|1=Kikuzumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683022}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]]. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "[[:en:Matsumura Goshun|Goshun]]" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * [[:en:Japan National Route 176|National Route 176 (Japan)]] * [[:en:Japan National Route 173|National Route 173 (Japan)]] * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Mount Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:能勢街道]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11611938}}
