This new route closely aligns with today’s National Route 176. Modern infrastructure, such as {{ill|Hankyu|en|Hankyu|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu Electric Railway|1=Hankyu}}'s {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} and National Routes 176 and 173, run parallel to the old road. The {{ill|Nose Electric Railway|en|Nose Electric Railway|WD=Q3743743|lt=Nose Electric Railway|1=Nose Electric Railway}}'s {{ill|Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|ja|能勢電鉄妙見線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Electric Railway Myōken Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōken Line|WD=Q15223648}} also parallels the highway north of {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|en|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|WD=Q609350|lt=Kawanishi, Hyōgo|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}. These developments led to the decline of the old highway, though much of its path remains as residential roads. Commercial hubs such as Okamachi and the area around {{ill|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|en|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|WD=Q866078|lt=Ishibashi Handai-mae Station|1=Ishibashi handai-mae Station}} have preserved the historical route as shopping streets. The {{ill|Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja|阪神淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Hanshin Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阪神・淡路大震災|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} severely damaged many old wooden buildings along the route. Unfortunately, some of the historic architecture was replaced or demolished, and many sites have been converted into parking lots. == Inns Along the Highway == * {{ill|Hattori, Toyonaka|ja|服部 (豊中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hattori, Toyonaka|1=Hattori, Toyonaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516871}} * {{ill|Ishibashi, Osaka|ja|石橋 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi, Osaka|1=Q11586621|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586621}} (Segawa-shuku), a junction with the {{ill|Saigoku Kaidō|ja|西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigoku Kaidō|1=Seigoku kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518413}} * Ikeda == Works Featuring Nose Kaidō == * The [[rakugo]] story "{{ill|Ikeda no Inokai|ja|池田の猪買い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Inokai|1=Q11551659|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551659}}" (''Buying Wild Boar in Ikeda'')
({{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/Hollywood/2975/sub16.html |title=Referenced |date=20011218151505}}). == Famous Products Along the Nose Kaidō == * '''Ikeda Charcoal''' – Known for its chrysanthemum-like cross-section, called "{{ill|Kikuzumi|ja|菊炭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuzumi|1=Q28683022|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683022}}" for tea ceremonies. It was once favored by {{ill|Sen no Rikyū|en|Sen no Rikyū|WD=Q305477|lt=Sen no Rikyū|1=Sen no Rikyū}}. * '''Sake''' – Ikeda’s "{{ill|Matsumura Goshun|en|Matsumura Goshun|WD=Q960116|lt=Goshun|1=Matsumura Goshun}}" is especially renowned. * '''Boar Meat''' – Ikeda was once famous for boar meat, as featured in the rakugo story "Ikeda no Inokai." Although wild boars still inhabit the region, the meat has lost its status as a local specialty. == See Also == * {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|WD=Q11421416|lt=National Route 176 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 176}} * {{ill|Japan National Route 173|en|Japan National Route 173|WD=Q11421415|lt=National Route 173 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 173}} * {{ill|Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōken (Osaka and Hyōgo)|1=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447024}} * {{ill|Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myōkendo (Nichiren-shū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.apsara.ne.jp/hattori-tenjingu/ Hattori Tenjingu Shrine] * [http://con-bat.sakura.ne.jp/michi-kawa/album/index2.html#1 Kinki Road Stories] (Radio Osaka site) * [https://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/machi/toshikeikan/hyakei/field/omomuki/kyunosekaidou.html Old Nose Kaidō near Okamachi] (Toyonaka City site) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|能勢街道|version=102374857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11611938|ja|能勢街道}} [[Category:Roads in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Roads in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Streets in Japan]] [[Category:Transportation history of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Transportation history of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
